shell.brandDescription: Operational control for documents, risks, audits, CAPA, reviews, KPIs, and training.

page.label: Start Here
page.title: Implementation and readiness path
page.description: Use this page as the first stop for tenant setup: review the core readiness checklist, then use the optional implementation workspace when guided rollout is needed.

feedback.loadingCopy: Bringing in tenant rollout settings and objective guidance.
feedback.noWriteWorkspace: You do not have permission to update the implementation workspace.
feedback.noWriteChecklist: You do not have permission to update the rollout checklist.
feedback.loadError: Implementation workspace could not be loaded.
feedback.saveError: Implementation workspace could not be saved.

intro.title: Start here
intro.copy: Use this page to orient a new tenant quickly. The readiness checklist stays available for everyone with workspace access, and the implementation workspace can remain optional for mature companies.

readiness.title: Core setup checklist
readiness.copy: Walk through these essentials in order so the tenant has a clear first-use path before deeper rollout work begins.

companyProfile.label: Company profile
companyProfile.hint: Confirm tenant identity, numbering defaults, and system setup.

usersRoles.label: Users and roles
usersRoles.hint: Set access, ownership, and who will operate each module.

processMap.label: Process map
processMap.hint: Define the main processes and how they connect across the system.

controlledDocuments.label: Controlled documents
controlledDocuments.hint: Load the policies, procedures, forms, and work instructions needed for operation.

risks.label: Risks
risks.hint: Capture key business, compliance, QHSE, and operational risks early.

audits.label: Audits
audits.hint: Set the audit programme and prepare how effectiveness will be checked.

ncrCapaActions.label: NCR/CAPA/actions
ncrCapaActions.hint: Make sure nonconformities, corrective action, and follow-up ownership are ready.

kpis.label: KPIs
kpis.hint: Define the measures and thresholds that show whether the system is working.

managementReview.label: Management review
managementReview.hint: Prepare the leadership review path for decisions, resources, and improvement.

guidance.flowCopy: Company profile -> Users -> Processes -> Documents -> Risks -> Audits -> NCR/CAPA -> KPIs -> Management Review.
guidance.hiddenTitle: The implementation workspace is currently hidden for this tenant.
guidance.hiddenCopy: That is useful for companies that already have ISO implemented and only need the operational modules. This Start Here page still stays available for orientation, and you can turn the deeper rollout workspace back on later in

profile.title: Rollout profile
profile.copy: Keep the setup context short and visible so the tenant knows how this workspace is being used.

checklist.title: Rollout checkpoints
checklist.copy: High-level setup only. This should stay lighter than a project plan and simply show whether the basics are in place.

items.scopeContext: Define scope, context, and interested parties
items.policyDocuments: Approve policies and controlled document structure
items.objectivesKpis: Set objectives, targets, and KPI review ownership
items.processRisk: Map processes and assess key risks, hazards, and aspects
items.operationsTraining: Deploy operational controls, training, and provider controls
items.auditReview: Run internal audit and hold management review

objective.title: Objective establishment starter
objective.copy: Keep one practical starter objective here, then move the live measure and readings into KPIs.

placeholders.statement: Improve on-time supplier approval and reduce traceability gaps by year end.

pdca.title: Recommended module sequence
pdca.copy: Use PDCA as a suggested rollout order, not as another register to keep updating.

Plan.label: Plan
Plan.summary: Define the management system direction before pushing records into daily use.

Do.label: Do
Do.summary: Deploy controls, competence, and supplier management into daily operations.

Check.label: Check
Check.summary: Use evidence, incidents, and audits to test whether the system is performing as intended.

Act.label: Act
Act.summary: Drive improvement from findings, failures, changes, and management decisions.

modules.Context: Scope, issues, interested parties, and needs

page.label: Dashboard
page.title: Integrated Management System
page.description: A structured operational workspace for quality, environmental, and health and safety management.

watchlist.title: Areas needing attention
watchlist.copy: A quick view of the modules that currently need review.

breakdown.title: {{module}} breakdown
breakdown.copy: A compact view of the {{metric}} behind the main {{chart}}.

page.label: Clause 4
page.title: Context of Organization
page.description: Manage the issues, parties, and expectations that shape the management system.

empty.noAccessCopy: Your current role does not include context.read.
empty.noIssuesCopy: Start by adding internal or external issues.

headlines.open: Open context issues still need active review

narratives.open: Use the open issues as management inputs. Where needed, continue into risk assessment or process review so the issue has visible follow-up.
narratives.monitoring: Monitoring issues remain visible for review and can still be escalated into risks or process changes if the situation worsens.
narratives.stable: Recent context records are either resolved or not currently driving immediate management attention.

areas.title: Open one working area at a time
areas.copy: Choose the register you want to review, then work inside that area without losing the Clause 4 view.

internalIssues.label: Internal issues
internalIssues.copy: Operational, organizational, and culture factors inside the business.

externalIssues.label: External issues
externalIssues.copy: Regulatory, supplier, market, and stakeholder influences on the IMS.

interestedParties.label: Interested parties
interestedParties.copy: Customers, regulators, employees, suppliers, and other key stakeholders.

needs.label: Needs & expectations
needs.copy: The requirements and expectations that should stay visible in planning and review.

recent.title: Latest context records
recent.copy: Review the latest internal and external issues, then continue into risk or process follow-up where needed.

errors.load: Context dashboard could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: Review controlled documents and move them through a simple lifecycle: Draft, Review, Approved, and Obsolete.
descriptions.create: Create a controlled document and prepare it for review, approval, and future revision control.
descriptions.detail: Review document details, confirm lifecycle status, and keep evidence and actions together with the record.
descriptions.edit: Update document details without losing the controlled lifecycle and revision history.

list.title: Controlled document register
list.copy: Controlled documents are the live policies, procedures, forms, and instructions people use to run the system every day.
list.filtersCopy: Search by code, title, or type, then open a record for review or revision control.
list.emptyTitle: No documents match the current filter
list.emptyCopy: Adjust the search or create the first controlled document for this tenant.

form.copy: Set the document identity, owner, and lifecycle here. Evidence and actions stay in dedicated panels once the record exists.
form.title: Title

detail.lifecycleCopy: Move the record through review, approval, and obsolescence without opening the editor.
detail.evidenceCopy: Keep supporting files and related actions with the controlled record so lifecycle review and revision history stay easy to follow.

detailLifecycle.draft: Next control point: complete the revision note, assign ownership, and move the document into review when the draft is ready.
detailLifecycle.reviewWithApprove: Next control point: review the evidence trail and approve the document when the content and metadata are complete.
detailLifecycle.reviewWithoutApprove: Next control point: confirm evidence and revision details, then hand the document to an approver with documents.approve permission.
detailLifecycle.approved: Next control point: maintain review due dates and linked actions, and mark the record obsolete rather than deleting it when superseded.
detailLifecycle.obsolete: Next control point: keep the obsolete record for traceability and make sure the replacement live document is available in the register.

nextSteps.approved: Next: review evidence, confirm linked actions, and keep the review due date current.
nextSteps.review: Next: review the lifecycle step, confirm evidence is complete, and move the document toward approval.
nextSteps.default: Next: confirm lifecycle details, review evidence, and keep linked actions up to date.

reviewDue.overdueCopy: This controlled document passed its review due date on {{date}}. Review the content, confirm relevance, and either revise or reapprove it.
reviewDue.soonCopy: This document is due for review on {{date}}. Confirm the owner, evidence, and revision note before the review date passes.

messages.noPermission: You do not have permission to update documents.
messages.confirmDeleteDraft: Delete this draft document? Approved and obsolete documents cannot be deleted.
messages.confirmObsolete: Mark this approved document as obsolete? This preserves traceability instead of deleting the record.

hints.draft: Use Draft while the content is being prepared. This is the working version before review and approval.
hints.review: Use Review when the content is ready to be checked and approved. Keep the revision note and supporting evidence current before approval.
hints.approved: Approved is the controlled live version in use. Keep the effective date and review due date current so periodic review stays visible.
hints.obsolete: Obsolete keeps the old version available for traceability after it has been replaced by a new live document.

descriptions.list: A calmer register for assessed risks and opportunities, ownership, target follow-up, and linked actions.
descriptions.create: Capture a new risk or opportunity in a dedicated page without mixing the register and editor.
descriptions.detail: Review the assessment, current actions, and evidence in one focused detail view.
descriptions.edit: Update the assessment and action plan in a dedicated edit workflow.

list.title: Risk register
list.copy: A clear enterprise register for risks, opportunities, treatment ownership, linked actions, and live scoring.
list.filtersCopy: Search by title or category, then open the record for assessment, actions, and evidence.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing current assessment and treatment data.
list.emptyTitle: No records match the current filter
list.emptyCopy: Adjust the search or create the first risk or opportunity entry for this tenant.

form.stillLinkedTitle: Still linked to the source issue
form.title: Title
form.starterCategoryHint: Starter categories switch with {{entity}} type. You can still enter a custom category.

intro.risk: Separate the initial risk, mitigation planning, and residual risk review so the assessment reads like a real IMS risk register.
intro.opportunity: Separate the initial opportunity, realization actions, and residual opportunity review so the record is easy to follow.

likelihoodHint.opportunity: Scale 1-5: 1 unlikely to realize, 5 very likely to realize.

detail.evidenceCopy: Keep the source issue, process context, mitigation actions, and supporting evidence aligned to this record.

page.descriptionList: Manage internal and supplier audits with clear planning, checklist execution, findings, and follow-up.
page.descriptionCreate: Set up the audit plan, agenda, and meeting notes first, then move into the checklist.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the audit plan, complete the checklist, decide the next step for each finding, and finish the close-out.
page.descriptionEdit: Update audit metadata without mixing it with execution details.

actions.saveAndPrepareChecklist: Save audit and prepare AI checklist

list.title: Internal and supplier audits
list.copy: Plan audits, work through the checklist, record findings, and decide the right follow-up in one place.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing current audit plans, checklist progress, and findings.

form.copy: Set the audit plan first. Internal audits can preload ISO-based questions, while supplier audits can stay simple and custom.

placeholders.title: Purchasing process audit
placeholders.scope: Process, site, supplier, or function under audit

errors.selectStandard: Select an ISO standard for internal audits.

nextSteps.default: Next: continue the audit from the current workflow step.
nextSteps.completed: Next: review the completed audit record, its findings, and the formal report output.
nextSteps.review: Next: review findings, complete the close-out, and then finish the audit from the review step.
nextSteps.conduct: Next: continue the checklist, record findings where needed, and return to the review step when the checklist is complete.

workflow.step1: Step 1: confirm scope and audit setup
workflow.step3Findings: Step 3: decide the next step for each finding

workflowCopy.openRecord: Select an audit to continue the workflow.
workflowCopy.completed: The audit record is already complete. You can still review findings, linked CAPA, lighter audit actions, and the final PDF report.
workflowCopy.plan: Check the programme, scope, objectives, criteria, and meeting notes first. Once the setup reads correctly, start the audit and move into the checklist.
workflowCopy.conduct: Work through the checklist question by question. Record findings only where the requirement is not met, then keep moving through the audit.
workflowCopy.review: Stay in the findings trail until every gap has a clear next step. Minor and major findings move into CAPA; observations and opportunities can use a lighter audit action or close directly.
workflowCopy.closeout: All findings have a clear next step. Record the conclusion, recommendations, completion date, and auditor to finish the audit without reopening the checklist.

plan.title: Programme, scope, and readiness
plan.copy: Confirm the audit programme, scope type, criteria, agenda, and meeting structure before starting fieldwork.

empty.openingMeeting: No opening meeting notes prepared yet.
empty.closingMeeting: No closing meeting notes prepared yet.

pack.internalCopy: Use the programme, agenda, and opening or closing notes to issue the audit properly before the checklist starts.
pack.supplierCopy: Supplier audits should still start with a simple agenda, opening discussion, and planned close-out summary before questions are assessed.

annualProgramme.title: Annual audit programme
annualProgramme.copy: Use the programme field to show which annual plan or audit cycle this audit belongs to before it moves into execution.

scopePlanning.title: Department, process, or whole-company planning
scopePlanning.copy: The scope type makes it explicit whether the audit is company-wide, department-based, process-based, site-based, or supplier-focused.

meetingStructure.title: Meeting structure
meetingStructure.copy: Opening and closing meeting notes let the audit record show how the agenda was issued, how the audit was opened, and how the results were closed out.

conduct.copy: Answer each question in order. If a requirement is not met, choose No, record the finding, then continue the audit from the same question.
conduct.readOnlyCopy: This audit has been completed. You can still review findings and evidence, but checklist responses can no longer be changed.

currentClauseHelper.supplier: Answer each supplier audit question, record evidence, and add a finding only when a requirement is not met.
currentClauseHelper.internal: Answer each question. If a requirement is not met, record a finding and continue to the next question.

conduct.emptyInternalCopy: Checklist questions will appear here once the audit template is available.
conduct.emptySupplierCopy: Add the first supplier audit question to begin the audit.

completion.title: Next step: review findings
completion.copy: All checklist questions are answered. Open Review findings now, decide the next step for each gap, then complete the audit close-out last.

findingNextStep.needed: Record the finding before moving on so the failed requirement has a complete audit trail.
findingNextStep.closed: This finding route is already closed for the question. Review it if needed, or continue with the next question.
findingNextStep.capaLinked: Finding and CAPA are already linked. Review the linked CAPA or continue with the next question.
findingNextStep.capaNeeded: Finding recorded. CAPA still needs to be raised for this nonconformity before the route is complete.
findingNextStep.lightRoute: Finding recorded. Review the lighter follow-up route or continue with the next question.

findingPrompt.titleClosed: Finding route is already closed for this question
findingPrompt.titleCapaLinked: Finding and CAPA already linked for this question
findingPrompt.titleCapaNeeded: Finding recorded: CAPA still needed for this question
findingPrompt.copyNeeded: The No answer has been recorded. Now capture the finding title, gap description, and severity so the review step has a complete audit trail.
findingPrompt.copyClosed: This failed answer already has a finding with a closed route. Use Review Finding if you need to reopen that trail before continuing.
findingPrompt.copyCapaLinked: This failed answer already has a linked finding and CAPA. Use Review Finding or Open CAPA if you need to return to that trail before continuing.
findingPrompt.copyCapaNeeded: This failed answer already has a finding, but the nonconformity still needs CAPA before the route is complete. Use Review Finding to continue that decision.
findingPrompt.copyLightRoute: This failed answer already has a lighter finding route. Use Review Finding if you need to review action or closure before continuing.

findingModal.title: Record audit finding
findingModal.descriptionHelper: Write the real situation first, then use AI only if you want help turning it into cleaner audit wording.
findingModal.checklistCopy: 1. Write a short note in your own words. 2. Use AI only if you want cleaner wording. 3. Choose severity. 4. Save the finding. 5. Decide the next step later in Review findings.

review.title: Finish finding routes, then close the audit
review.copy: Keep the chain simple: review each finding, decide the next step inside that finding, then record the audit close-out last.
review.incompleteCopy: Answer all required checklist questions before the audit can move into final completion.

focus.default: Current focus: review each finding one at a time
focus.copyDefault: Select a finding, decide whether it should close, move into an audit action, or move into CAPA, then continue to the next finding.

guidance.routeComplete: Route is complete in the audit record.
guidance.continueLinkedCapa: Continue the linked CAPA, then return here only when you need to review closure.
guidance.linkedAction: A lighter audit action is already linked to this finding.
guidance.actionPrepared: Lighter follow-up is already prepared for this finding below.
guidance.capaNeeded: This finding needs CAPA before the next step is complete.
guidance.lightRoute: Choose a lighter action or close the finding directly when satisfied.

review.traceabilityCopy: Follow the chain from checklist evidence to finding, then into {{capaText}} and {{actionText}}.

guidance.completeChecklist: Complete all checklist questions before the audit can be finished.
guidance.unresolvedFindings: Each finding still needs a clear next step before this audit should move into final close-out.
guidance.completeFields: Record the conclusion, recommendations, completion date, and auditor before finishing the audit.
guidance.ready: The audit record can be completed now. Findings, actions, and NCR follow-up can continue after audit completion without reopening the audit itself.

attention.needsAttention: This audit currently needs management attention.
attention.underControl: This audit is currently under control.
attention.loading: Attention guidance appears after the audit is loaded.
attention.controlledCopy: Audit timing, checklist execution, and findings follow-up are controlled enough for routine oversight.

page.label: Assurance
page.title: Actions
page.description: Track assigned follow-up from any module in one place.

focused.copy: Review the action details above, then update status or return to the source record.

register.title: Assigned follow-up
register.copy: Track open work, ownership, and due dates from audits, CAPA, incidents, risks, and other modules.

empty.loadingCopy: Refreshing assigned follow-up, owners, and due dates.
empty.noneTitle: No actions match the current filters
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the filters or open a source record to create a new action.

headlines.ownerNeeded: Some actions still need an owner
headlines.active: Follow-up is active across the system
headlines.underControl: Assigned follow-up is currently under control

narratives.overdue: Start with overdue actions first. They represent assigned follow-up that has already missed the committed due date.
narratives.ownerNeeded: Some actions are still open or in progress without a named owner. Assign responsibility before the next review cycle so follow-up does not stall.
narratives.active: Use this page to track assigned follow-up from any module so ownership, due dates, and status stay visible in one place.
narratives.underControl: Current action records are either complete or not yet needing extra intervention.

headlines.needsAttention: This action currently needs management attention.
headlines.underControl: This action is currently under control.

narratives.underControl: The action has either been completed, cancelled, or still has enough control around due date and ownership.

messages.noPermissionUpdate: You do not have permission to update actions.
messages.deleteConfirm: Delete action "{{title}}"? Completed actions are protected.

page.descriptionList: Use NCR to record the nonconformance, who owns it, what follow-up is needed, and whether verification is complete.
page.descriptionCreate: Capture the nonconformance first, then continue the investigation and corrective trail from the record.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the nonconformance, investigation, follow-up actions, verification, comments, attachments, and activity.
page.descriptionEdit: Update the NCR while keeping the investigation and verification trail clear.

list.title: Nonconformance log
list.copy: NCR records the nonconformance first, then keeps the investigation, action follow-up, and verification trail together.
list.filtersCopy: Filter by status, category, source, severity, or owner.

empty.noAccessCopy: Your current role does not include ncr.read.
empty.noneTitle: No NCR records match the current filter
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the filters or create the first NCR entry for this tenant.

placeholders.title: Unapproved process change
placeholders.description: Describe what happened and what requirement was missed

detail.workflowCopy: Work from investigation to task follow-up and then formal verification before closure.

guidance.ownerFilterDefault: Select a process if you want to suggest the process owner as the most likely NCR owner.
guidance.ownerFilterScoped: Owner options are narrowed to {{owner}} from {{process}}.
guidance.ownerFilterNoOwner: This process does not have an assigned process owner yet, so all users remain available.

nextSteps.create: Next: open the saved NCR and continue with investigation, actions, and verification from the record.
nextSteps.edit: Next: continue the NCR from the relevant workflow tab.
nextSteps.initial: Next: continue the investigation, capture root cause, and then move into corrective actions.
nextSteps.actioning: Next: review corrective actions, keep comments and evidence current, and prepare for verification.
nextSteps.verification: Next: complete verification and confirm whether the NCR can be closed or needs more action.
nextSteps.closed: Next: review the investigation trail, linked actions, and evidence as part of the NCR record.

guidance.initial: Capture containment first, then record enough investigation detail to justify why the NCR is moving into root cause analysis.
guidance.investigation: Complete the investigation summary and root cause before moving fully into corrective action ownership.
guidance.correctiveAction: Keep corrective action summary, ownership, comments, and evidence current so the NCR is ready for verification.
guidance.verification: Use verification to confirm the corrective action was effective, not just completed. Reject verification if the issue can still recur.
guidance.closed: Keep the NCR record available as evidence of containment, root cause, corrective action, and verification outcome.
guidance.archived: Archived NCRs remain available for traceability and should not be treated as active issues.

guidance.verified: The corrective action has been confirmed as effective. Keep the verifier and verification date visible in the record.
guidance.rejected: Use rejected when the corrective action did not prevent recurrence. Return to investigation or action in progress with updated evidence.
guidance.pending: Pending means the action may be implemented, but effectiveness has not yet been confirmed.

followUp.overdueCopy: The due date passed on {{dueDate}}. Review corrective actions, verification status, and owner accountability before the NCR drifts further.
followUp.approachingCopy: The due date is {{dueDate}}. Confirm the investigation, root cause, and corrective action trail before the due date arrives.
followUp.verificationHeading: Verification still needs a formal result
followUp.verificationCopy: This NCR is pending verification. Record who verified the action and whether the result was accepted or rejected.

headlines.needsAttention: This NCR currently needs management attention.
headlines.underControl: This NCR is currently under control.

narratives.unsaved: Attention guidance appears after the NCR is saved.
narratives.underControl: Ownership, due date, verification state, and current NCR stage are controlled enough for routine follow-up.

messages.deleteConfirmCurrent: Delete this NCR? Closed records should be archived instead.
messages.loadListFailed: NCR log could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailFailed: NCR details could not be loaded.
messages.loadCommentsFailed: NCR comments could not be loaded.
messages.loadActivityFailed: NCR activity could not be loaded.

page.descriptionList: Use CAPA for formal corrective action: define the issue, confirm root cause, assign action, verify effectiveness, and close with evidence.
page.descriptionCreate: Create a CAPA record for issues that need formal corrective action and tracked closure.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the problem, root cause, actions, verification, and closure evidence in one place.
page.descriptionEdit: Update the CAPA record while keeping the corrective-action trail clear.

list.title: CAPA register
list.copy: CAPA manages root cause, corrective action, verification, and closure after a significant issue is raised.
list.filtersCopy: Search by title or source, then open the record for cause analysis, action follow-up, and closure evidence.

empty.loadingCopy: Refreshing nonconformity, corrective action, and closure status.
empty.noneTitle: No CAPA records match the current filter
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the search or create the first CAPA entry for this tenant.

form.copy: Keep the problem statement, cause, actions, and closure inputs in clearly separated sections.

headline.OPEN: Start by defining the issue clearly
headline.IN_PROGRESS: Keep ownership and due dates visible
headline.VERIFIED: Confirm the action worked before closure
headline.CLOSED: Closure should be supported by a clear summary

copy.OPEN: Record the problem clearly so the team understands what happened and why formal corrective action is needed.
copy.INVESTIGATING: Containment and root cause should be clear before the CAPA moves into action planning.
copy.ACTION_PLANNED: The corrective response should now be clear enough to assign, track, and review.
copy.IN_PROGRESS: Once actions are underway, owner accountability and due-date control become the main focus.
copy.VERIFIED: Verification should confirm that the action was effective, not only completed.
copy.CLOSED: A closed CAPA should explain what evidence supports closure and why recurrence is now controlled.

hint.assigned: Owner already assigned. Keep the due date and verification method aligned with the selected status.
hint.unassigned: Assign an owner once the CAPA moves beyond initial definition so accountability is visible.

actionControl.noOwnerNoDate: Assign an owner and due date once the CAPA moves into action planning so follow-up is visible.
actionControl.noOwner: Due date is set, but owner is still missing. Assign ownership before the CAPA relies on action follow-up.
actionControl.noDueDate: Owner is assigned, but due date is still missing. Add a due date so the CAPA can be monitored properly.
actionControl.ready: Owner and due date are set. Keep them aligned with the current CAPA stage and any linked tasks.

headline.needsAttention: This CAPA currently needs management attention.
headline.underControl: This CAPA is currently under control.

narrative.unsaved: Attention guidance appears after the CAPA is saved.
narrative.underControl: Ownership, due date, verification, and current CAPA stage are controlled enough for routine follow-up.

messages.noPermission: You do not have permission to update CAPA records.
messages.deleteConfirm: Delete this CAPA? Closed CAPA records cannot be deleted.
messages.loadListFailed: CAPA register could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailFailed: CAPA details could not be loaded.
